regulation_clause.text: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once in every two years for high risk customers, once in every eight years for medium risk customers and once in every ten years for low risk customers from the date of opening of the account or the date of last KYC updation.

gold_rationale: KYCUPD.cbl fixes the periodic re-KYC interval at 10 years for every customer via WS-REFRESH-YEARS, but clause 38 of the KYC Directions, 2025 (effective 2025-11-28) requires high-risk customers to undergo KYC updation at least once every two years. The 10-year constant matches only the low-risk tier, so high- and medium-risk accounts are refreshed against a stale threshold.
